How to get Email id also from the UME thru url?

Thanks,

Senthil

entry.title: update shop Order by WS call ShopOrderServiceWS from PCo
entry.content:
Hello Together.
I try to update a shopOrder by calling the ShopOrderServiceWS in 2 steps.
1. readShopOrder to get the modifiedDateTime
2. updateShopOrder with modifiedDateTime

There seems to be an issue with the modifiedDateTime in PCO. In the xml the format is "2024-11-27T11:08.000+01:00". The PCO-Datatype is System.dateTime. When using the read datetime of step1 the value is outModifiedDateTime [System.DateTime]: 2024-11-27T10:08:01Z.
It looks like there gets the information "+01:00" lost. The Answer is always "Message 100006".
When I try the same in the WSNavigatior it already works well...
Does anybody has a hint for me?

entry.title: Reduce Risk, Increase Adoption: Leveraging SAP SuccessFactors Process Library
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced digital world, HR transformation is not just an IT project but a strategic initiative that can significantly enhance business operations. Implementing a new HR solution like SAP SuccessFactors should aim to simplify processes, increase efficiency, and improve employee engagement. One of the key tools to achieve these goals is the SAP SuccessFactors process library.

This blog post was created based on the content presented at the SAP SuccessFactors Services webinar Reduce Risk, Increase Adoption: Leveraging SAP SuccessFactors Process Library for Effective HR Transformation delivered on Thursday 21st November, 2024 by HR Business Architects Maria Cicciopastore, Marijn van Amelsfort, and Karlo Smojver. 

Why Cloud Solutions are Different

Cloud-based HR solutions like SAP SuccessFactors offer numerous advantages over traditional IT implementations. They provide faster implementation, scalability, and continuous innovation. However, they also come with limitations, such as the inability to customize the software extensively. Instead, organizations must focus on configuring the solution to meet their needs. This shift necessitates a new approach to process design and implementation.

The Role of the SAP SuccessFactors process library

The SAP SuccessFactors Process Library is a comprehensive collection of leading practices and process diagrams developed by SAP experts. It serves as a valuable resource for organizations embarking on HR transformation projects. Here are some key benefits of using the process library:

  1. Jumpstart Transformations: The library provides a starting point for design discussions, helping organizations avoid starting from scratch. By using leading practices, companies can quickly identify fit-gap areas and streamline their processes.

  2. User-Centric Design: The process library emphasizes the importance of keeping the user at the center of process design. This approach ensures that the new HR solution is intuitive and easy to use for all employees, not just HR professionals.

  3. Standardization and Harmonization: By adopting standardized processes, organizations can achieve greater consistency and efficiency across their HR operations. The process library helps in harmonizing processes, making them easier to manage and scale.

How to Get Started

To effectively leverage the SAP SuccessFactors process library,  SAP Services support the customers with the following structured approach:

  1. Preparation: Set up the SAP Signavio environment and upload the latest process library release. Invite stakeholders to review and comment on the diagrams to ensure everyone is aligned before the workshops.
  2. Strategy: Understand what are the key objectives of the transformation, the value driver, and the design principle to follow
  3. Process Design Workshops: Conduct workshops with key stakeholders, including HR teams, business leaders, IT professionals, and external partners. Use the process library as a foundation to design to-be processes that meet business requirements, and use the value drivers and design principle as a guardrail. 
  4. Fit-Gap Analysis: Perform a fit-gap analysis to identify areas where the standard processes need to be adjusted to fit the organization's unique needs, in alignment with the customer strategy. This step helps in the early adoption of best practices and reduces implementation risks.
  5. Consolidation and Handover: After the workshops, consolidate the designed processes and prepare for implementation. Use SAP Signavio's reporting capabilities to document business requirements, key decisions, and changes

Real-World Example

A recent example of leveraging the SAP SuccessFactors Process Library involved a customer facing challenges with their HR transformation. They had a poorly implemented SuccessFactors solution and were unsure about their readiness for additional modules. By conducting a Phase Zero readiness project using the process library, they were able to:

  • Conduct a thorough fit-gap analysis.
  • Gain insights into future HR roles and responsibilities.
  • Clarify business requirements and prepare for the next implementation phase.

This approach empowered the customer to proceed confidently with their HR transformation journey.

Conclusion

The SAP SuccessFactors process library is a powerful tool for organizations looking to reduce risks and increase adoption during HR transformation projects. By leveraging leading practices and a user-centric design approach, companies can achieve faster, more efficient implementations and ultimately enhance their HR operations. Whether you're just starting your HR transformation or looking to optimize your existing processes, the process library offers valuable resources to guide you every step of the way.

Useful links

  • SAP SuccessFactors process library in SAP Signavio:  https://community.sap.com/t5/process-diagrams-in-sap-signavio/sap-successfactors-process-library-in-sap-signavio/ta-p/216161
  • Webinar Recording:   https://play.goconsensus.com/s618489ab
  • Slides:  https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/xkRwLp6?rc=10&doi=SAP1146726

Feel free to share this blog post on the SAP SuccessFactors community pages to help other customers understand the benefits and best practices of using the SAP SuccessFactors Process Library for their HR transformation projects. If you have any questions or need further assistance, don't hesitate to reach out!

entry.title: Dump CONVT_NO_NUMBER during edition smartform
entry.content:
Good morning,

Since couple of days, I have difficulties to modify specific smartforms trough transaction code SMARTFORMS.

Indeed, when I go in change mode on a specific smartform, then I select a node type "TEXT" and text type is "Text Element", then I add a variable like &GV_MASK&, I click on the check button and then I got a dump ( see attachment ) with the the following details :

Category               ABAP Programming Error
Runtime Errors         CONVT_NO_NUMBER
Except.                CX_SY_CONVERSION_NO_NUMBER
ABAP Program           SAPLSTXK
Application Component  BC-SRV-SCR
Date and Time          28.11.2024 10:23:15

 Short Text
     "15&GV_MASK&" cannot be interpreted as a number

 What happened?
     Error in the ABAP Application Program

     The current ABAP program "SAPLSTXK" had to be terminated because it has
     come across a statement that unfortunately cannot be executed.

 What can you do?
     Note down which actions and inputs caused the error.

     To process the problem further, contact you SAP system
     administrator.

     Using Transaction ST22 for ABAP Dump Analysis, you can look
     at and manage termination messages, and you can also
     keep them for a long time.

 Error analysis
     An exception has occurred which is explained in more detail below. The
     exception is assigned to class 'CX_SY_CONVERSION_NO_NUMBER' and was not caught
      in procedure
     "RTF_SET_PATTERN" "(FORM)", nor was it propagated by a RAISING clause.
     Since the caller of the procedure could not have anticipated this
     exception, the current program was terminated.
     The reason for the exception is:
     An attempt was made to interpret value "15&GV_MASK&" as a number.
     As this value contravenes the rules for displaying numbers correctly,
     this was not possible.

I do not know why I have this dump.

Can you please support quite urgently as I am unable to modify /correct smartforms which contains TEXT nodes.

Thank you for your support

Best regards,

Christophe

entry.title: Introducing Warehousing Concept in SAP S/4 HANA Supply Chain Execution
entry.content:
Hi All

I am explaining warehousing in Supply Chain Execution in SAP S/4 Hana

Brief Overview to SAP S/4 Hana and Features

With SAP S/4HANA, SAP is providing a product that represents the next generation of business applications. Simple enterprise software that is designed to help you run simple in the digital economy. SAP S/4HANA supports

Customer in dealing with challenges like integrating business data with data outside of our company and supporting our business processes with artificial intelligence (or AI). The SAP S/4HANA family is completely built on the in-memory platform SAP HANA. Using the potential of the SAP HANA platform, SAP S/4HANA is designed for customer digital business. It provides an instant insight into where your business currently stands. It does this by combining a single source of truth with dynamic planning and analysis capabilities and real-time process support.

With the SAP Fiori user experience and a less complex data model, SAP S/4HANA is designed to run simple. In parallel, it reduces the data footprint of your company.

 The SAP S/4HANA system is also already connected to business networks and company internal collaboration networks like SAP Build Work Zone. And it also prepares us for supporting your processes with SAP Business AI. All of these aspects protect your investment into SAP software by facilitating the next generation of business applications.

SAP S/4 Hana comes with following Products

SAP S/4 Hana on premises

SAP S/4 Hana Public Edition

SAP S/4 Hana Private Edition

SAP S/4HANA contains the below elements

SAP S/4HANA Enterprise Management.

SAP S/4HANA Line-of-Business Products.

SAP S/4HANA Line-of-Business (LoB) Products for specific industries.

SAP S/4HANA Compatibility Packs.

Supply Chain Management Capability in S/4 Hana

 

SAP S/4HANA enables companies to integrate and visualize all supply chain processes from supplier to customer. Intelligence can also be embedded in these processes. Digital supply chain and mission-critical business processes are supported across the enterprise with one single source of live information such as

  • Reduce Days of Inventory
  • Reduction in Revenue Loss Due to Fulfilment Issues
  • Reduced Overall Supply Chain Planning Costs
  • Improved Supply Chain Planning, increasing Productivity
  • Reduced TCO Through Landscape Simplification

Digital Supply Chain Execution

The core functionality provided by SAP S/4HANA can be extended with other (sometimes Cloud based) SAP products, to support the complete logistics network of a company.

Additional SAP products that support the complete supply chain

SAP Global Batch Traceability

SAP AI Core

SAP Yard Logistics

SAP User Experience Strategy

The use of mobile devices (smart-phones and tablets) to perform certain work tasks, is no longer reserved to a special group of experts. Ease of use is a very important characteristic for the end user of such a device. The focus of the user interface (UI) of the device is no longer maximum functionality, but rather a comfortable user experience and thus a focus on the consumer of the device. With this in mind, the business software running behind the device must be adapted and transformed so that it supports the user interface (used within the business roles of an enterprise) to a maximum. In practice this means that SAP S/4HANA and SAP Fiori as its user interface go hand-in-hand, and should be developed hand-in-hand.

Different User Types — Different Requirements

SAP Fiori

SAP Fiori is a collection of apps, which is created based on a specific set of guidelines and rules and that can be started using the SAP Fiori launchpad.

Available Warehouse Management Solutions in SAP S/4 Hana

Warehouse Management LE-WM

The classic SAP ERP solution for warehouse management LE-WM is part of SAP S/4HANA through a Compatibility Package (CP). This provides customers with a limited use right which expires on December 31, 2025. The inclusion of LE-WM in SAP S/4HANA helps customers migrating from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA to start using some of the advanced features of SAP S/4HANA without having to also immediately migrate their warehouse management solution at the same time. For existing customers that do not require complex warehouse processing in their SAP S/4HANA system, with release 1909 of SAP S/4HANA a basic warehouse management solution has been introduced in SAP S/4HANA called Stock Room Management.

Stock Room Management

The main reason for adding Stock Room Management to SAP S/4HANA is to give existing customers of LE-WM an opportunity to keep their existing LE-WM warehouses after 2025 that do not benefit immediately from moving to (embedded) SAP EWM. Stock Room Management is relevant for small warehouses with manual operations that manage stocks on storage bin level. this deployment allows customers to upgrade to SAP S/4HANA without performing a full-fledged WM transformation.

LE-WM features that are not supported in Stock Room Management include

  • Task and Resource Management (WM-TRM)
  • Warehouse Control Unit interface (WM-LSR)
  • Value Added Service (WM-VAS)
  • Yard Management (WM-YM)
  • Cross-Docking (WM-CD)
  • Wave Management (WM-TFM-CP)
  • Decentralized WM (WM-DWM)

SAP Extended Warehouse Management (SAP EWM)

SAP EWM first emerged as a successor to SAP Logistics Execution Warehouse Management (LE-WM), with added advantages and advanced features such as material flow system (MFS) and Labor Management. The earliest versions of SAP EWM were only available as separate standalone applications until 2016 when SAP embedded the EWM functionality in the  SAP S/4 Hana Suite. SAP EWM can be installed as standalone or be connected to an SAP S/4HANA system.

The solution can be deployed as Embedded or Decentral. In both case for On Premise or running SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private Edition.

Embedded EWM in SAP S/4HANA

Embedded SAP EWM is also available. Embedded means that no separate system is required and therefore SAP EWM can be used as a direct replacement for LE-WM. EWM is in such a set-up completely embedded within the SAP S/4HANA system. By embedding EWM on SAP S/4HANA, clients could take advantage of the SAP HANA in-memory database  and execute high-speed transactions for their warehousing operations. With each new version of SAP S/4HANA, more functionality was added to embedded EWM, including an ever-growing list of innovative SAP Fiori apps and a stripped-down version called stock room management.

The license type differentiation for Basic or  Advanced is only applicable for embedded deployment cases.

To Choose Basic or Advanced Please customized under IMG-EWM-Master Data -Acknowledge Use of Advance Function

Basic Extended Warehouse Management

Basic license type, customers are able to perform the main process end to end such as, Inbound, Outbound, Production Integration, Quality management integration, etc. Below you can find all the process included on basic license:


Inventory Management 

Inbound Processing 

Outbound Processing 

Internal Warehouse Movements

Physical Inventory

Reporting (Warehouse Management Monitor, Analytics)

Resource Management

Quality Management

Production Integration

Integration with Transportation Management (ASR)

IDOC Integration to third-party MFS systems (SAP Note 2873423)

Advanced Extended Warehouse Management

 The advanced license might be required in case of customer would use some features enclosed of the main process, for example, the outbound process designed is required to use Wave management or, the production integration is required to use JIT - Just in Time. It means that the Advanced license type is required in case of use some specific SAP EWM features, such as

Material Flow System (MFS)

Wave Management

Transportation units

Yard Management

Labor Management

Cross Docking

Value Added Services (VAS)

Warehouse Billing

Kitting

Dock Appointment Scheduling (DAS)

Slotting

Cartonization planning

Distribution Equipment

JIT

WIP Tracking

Warehouse Zones for advanced interleaving

License Management for SAP EWM Embedded

 The list of the extended objects for SAP EWM embedded is technically found on table /SCWM/EXT_OBJ. SAP Product Solution Team provide 2 reports for customers perform in their system in order to know which objects is being used on their warehouse system with usage detail

  • Report /SCWM/RP_COMPLIANCE_CHECK is available since S/4HANA 2016 (and higher) and provides the information if the customizing or the licensing  is compliance or non-compliance for each warehouse system.
  • Report /SCWM/RP_LICENCE_SELF_AUDIT is available since S/4HANA 2022 (and higher) and provides by warehouse number a view by month of usage of Basic and Advanced features by total number of items per month, number of average items per day and active working days, compliance or non-compliance from the customizing perspective

 Decentralized EWM in SAP S/4HANA

After SAP launched embedded EWM in SAP S/4HANA 1610, there were a lot of customers who realized that they wanted to use their existing systems but also embedded EWM features in another SAP S/4HANA physical server. The reasoning was this would ensure higher performance levels and warehouse business process continuity in case of any downtime. To cater to such a requirement, SAP introduced a new deployment option known as decentralized EWM in SAP S/4HANA 1809, FPS 01. Prior to this version, embedded EWM used to share the same master data objects which were loaded as part of the same SAP S/4HANA instance and could not interact with the master data of other SAP ERP  or SAP S/4HANA instances.  With the new decentralized deployment, clients can take advantage of current and new innovations in embedded EWM but keep on using separate SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA core systems.

Note- The Decentralize deployment always requires the advanced license type

Types of SAP EWM Warehouses

 

Types of warehouses and their main tasks could be

Production Warehouse

  • Raw-materials handling
  • Integration of quality management
  • Production supply
  • Receipts from production
  • Materials staging and consumption

Distribution Center

  • Complex process and high volume
  • High degree of automation
  • Wave management
  • Slotting and rearrangement
  • Replenishment

Service Parts Warehouse 

  • Low to very high complexity
  • Kitting and value-added services
  • High volatility
  • Serial numbering
  • Heterogeneous product ranges

Cross-Dock or Transit Warehouse 

  • Regional hubs, gateways, and container freight stations
  • Air and ocean freight handling
  • Unit-load devices and containers handling
  • Receive from and sent to airport or seaport (drayage)

Conclusion- It will give a brief idea about the products and warehouse Management which will help to utilize knowledge to choose correct option for warehouse in Supply chain Management.

Reference https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/understanding-license-type-basic-vs-advanced-for-sap-extended-warehouse/ba-p/13580547

SAP Help 

Please provide the feed back to improve it

entry.title: SAP Joule - Navigational Use Cases for custom fiori apps
entry.content:
Dear all,

I'm working on a RISE client how is asking to use SAP Joule capabilities. I've checked SAP Help Portal documentation and there is not any information about navigational use cases for custom Fiori Apps. Is possible to find custom Fiori Apps using SAP Joule? (Navigational Use Cases | SAP Help Portal) (I'm just trying to download files on this note 3523238 - Navigational and Transactional capabilities with Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 - SAP for Me but are not available)

For example, client has develop a custom Fiori app to 'Create purchase order for Spain department' that have a custom screen where few info is filled and then application is capable to generate a PO. 

In this case, client user execute prompt asking 'Let me know SAP Fiori application to create purchase order for Spain'. Is capable SAP Joule to inform about custom Fiori app or is only working for custom apps?

Thank you so much for your help.

BR,

entry.title: S4/HANA Business Objects for modules
entry.content:
Hello everyone! I need the most common business objects for the new S4/HANA business areas (modules), ChatGPT has developed the following, is this all correct (probably not) or do you know where I can reliably find this information? I can not find it on any website! Thanks in advance!

Einkauf (Procurement)

  • BUS2012: Purchase Order (Bestellung)
  • BUS2105: Purchase Requisition (Bestellanforderung)

Verkauf (Sales)

  • BUS2032: Sales Order (Kundenauftrag)
  • BUS2094: Quotation (Angebot)

Finanzwesen (Finance)

  • BUS2010: General Ledger Document (FI-Beleg)
  • BUS2093: Invoice (Rechnung)

Vermögensverwaltung (Asset Management)

  • BUS1022: Asset (Anlagegut)
  • BUS1013: Asset Transfer (Anlagenumbuchung)

Personalwesen (Human Resources)

  • BUS1065: Employee (Mitarbeiter)
  • BUS7023: Organizational Unit (Organisationseinheit)

Lieferkette (Supply Chain)

  • BUS3010: Handling Unit (Verpackungseinheit)
  • BUS4001: Outbound Delivery (Ausgangslieferung)

Forschung & Entwicklung, Konstruktion (Research & Development, Engineering)

  • BUS1080: Document Info Record (Dokumentenstammsatz)
  • BUS2090: Engineering Change Request (Änderungsanforderung)

Produktion (Manufacturing)

  • BUS1006: Material Master (Materialstammsatz)
  • BUS2005: Production Order (Fertigungsauftrag)

Wartung und Support (Service)

  • BUS2038: Maintenance Order (Instandhaltungsauftrag)
  • BUS2080: Service Notification (Servicemeldung)

Branchenspezifische Lösungen (Solutions for Specific Industries)

  • BUS2250: Oil & Gas Downstream Order (Branchenspezifischer Auftrag für Öl & Gas)
  • BUS1518: Healthcare Patient (Patientendaten)

Technische Module (Technical Modules)

  • BUS1170: Workflow Template (Workflow-Vorlage)
  • BUS1001: Business Partner (Geschäftspartner)

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

  • BUS2017: Event Subscription (für Event Mesh)
  • BUS4002: Cloud Integration Artifact (z. B. Integration Content)

Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Parallel Accounting and scope item
entry.content:
Question 1:-2VA and 1GA can only be added during the initial scoping phase of the project.

Question 2:- Solved: How to set up parallel accounting in S/4HANA Cloud... - SAP Community

Question 3:- Assign Accounting Principles to Ledgers and Company Codes | SAP Help Portal

Question 4:- Kindly go through the above help portal link.

Question 5:- Most of the scope items are default. The remaining scope items can be added via bundles through the 'Define Scope' activity.

Only non-standard scope items need to be requested by creating a ticket to the 'XX-S4C-OPR-SRV' component.

To check whether a scope item requires a license, please refer to the following Excel - Availability and Dependencies of Scope Items.xlsx .

entry.title: Personalized SAP Community RSS Feeds: Open Source Tool for Following Managed Tags, Contributor
entry.content:
Bringing Back RSS Feeds to the SAP Community

Do you miss the good old days when you could easily follow managed tags like "ABAP Development" and receive all the blog posts associated with that tag? Or when you could follow individual contributors via RSS? Well, I certainly did! That's why I decided to build my own RSS server to bring back this functionality and offer a SAP Community RSS Feed API!

The key advantage of this server is that it doesn’t rely on pre-generated feeds. Instead, you can customize and generate your own feed with precision.

Getting Started

For example, to follow the "ABAP Development" tag, you can use this URL:

https://rss-scn.marianzeis.de/api/messages?conversation.style=blog&managedTag.title=ABAP%20Development&feeds.replies=false

Want to include comments in your feed? Just set feeds.replies to true.

Interested only in Q&A? Use:

https://rss-scn.marianzeis.de/api/messages?conversation.style=qanda&managedTag.title=ABAP%20Development&feeds.replies=false

You can even follow specific users, like the amazing @qmacro . Simply use their user ID, for example:

https://rss-scn.marianzeis.de/api/messages?author.id=53

To simplify working with the parameters, I’ve provided a Swagger interface:

Swagger UI for SAP Community RSS Feed API

 

 

 

Features

This project is, of course, open source! Check it out on GitHub:

GitHub Repository: rss-scn

Some of the key features include:

  • Customize your RSS feeds by various parameters.
  • Follow specific tags, authors, or content types.
  • Include or exclude replies in your feeds.
  • Swagger documentation available at /api-docs.
  • Automatic deployment ensures the latest updates are always live.

How It Works

The SAP Community platform provides an API that allows access to its content. My RSS server acts as a bridge between this API and your RSS reader. When you make a request to my server with specific parameters, it fetches the relevant content from the SAP Community API, formats it into an RSS feed, and serves it back to you.

You don't need to be familiar with programming to use it. Just construct the URL with the parameters you want, and your RSS reader will handle the rest. The parameters allow you to filter content by author, tag, content type (like blogs or Q&A), and whether to include replies.

Examples of Usage

Let's say you're interested in all blog posts related to "ABAP Development" but don't want to see the replies. You'd use this URL:

https://rss-scn.marianzeis.de/api/messages?conversation.style=blog&managedTag.title=ABAP%20Development&feeds.replies=false

If you also want to include replies or comments, simply change feeds.replies to true:

https://rss-scn.marianzeis.de/api/messages?conversation.style=blog&managedTag.title=ABAP%20Development&feeds.replies=true

Interested in questions and answers instead of blogs? Adjust the conversation.style parameter:

https://rss-scn.marianzeis.de/api/messages?conversation.style=qanda&managedTag.title=ABAP%20Development&feeds.replies=false

To follow a specific author, use their author ID:

https://rss-scn.marianzeis.de/api/messages?author.id=53

Using the Swagger Interface

To make it even easier, there's a Swagger interface where you can interactively build your query without worrying about the exact URL syntax. You can access it here:

Swagger UI for SAP Community RSS Feed API

In the Swagger UI, you'll find a form where you can input your desired parameters. It will show you the exact URL to use and even let you test the API directly from the browser.

Creating an OPML File from Your SAP Community Following

If you want to import all the people you follow on SAP Community into your RSS reader, I've created a script that generates an OPML file—a format supported by most RSS readers for bulk import of feeds.

You can find the script and instructions here:

Create OPML from SAP Community Following

If you prefer, you can also use my sample OPML file, which includes a selection of popular contributors:

followers_feeds_sample.opml

Why I Built This

I was inspired by a post from Antonio Maradiaga titled "Exploring the RSS feeds of the new SAP Community platform". In his post, Antonio discusses how the new SAP Community platform has changed the way RSS feeds work, and some of the challenges in accessing them.

He pointed out that while the new platform offers some RSS capabilities, they are not as straightforward or flexible as they used to be. For instance, following individual contributors via RSS isn't readily available. You can read his full post here:

Exploring the RSS feeds of the new SAP Community platform

Wanting to bring back the convenience and customization of the old RSS feeds, I decided to create a solution that fills this gap. My RSS Feed API provides a more flexible way to access the content you care about.

Behind the Scenes

While you don't need to understand the technical details to use the service, here's a high-level overview of how it works:

  • API Integration: The server interacts with the SAP Community API to fetch the latest content based on your specified parameters.
  • Data Formatting: It takes the raw data from the API and formats it into an RSS feed, which is a standardized format that RSS readers can interpret.
  • Custom Parameters: By adjusting the query parameters in the URL, you can tailor the feed to include exactly what you're interested in—be it specific tags, authors, content types, or whether to include replies.
  • Open Source: The entire project is open source, meaning the code is publicly available for anyone to inspect, contribute to, or modify. This transparency ensures that the community can trust and improve the tool.
  • Automatic Updates: The server is set up to automatically update and deploy any changes, ensuring that you always have access to the latest features and fixes.

Conclusion

I hope you find this RSS Feed API as useful as I do! It's designed to bring back the flexibility and convenience of RSS feeds in the SAP Community, allowing you to stay updated with the content that matters most to you.

If you have any feedback, suggestions, or need assistance, feel free to reach out or contribute to the project on GitHub.

entry.title: Re: Vendor not getting selected for PR during MRP
entry.content:
Hi 

As per my understanding, It is mandatory to tick auto sourcing determine Vendor in PR automatically.''

see below blog here you will find logic for s/4 hana MRP and PIR 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/simplified-sourcing-simplification-in-s-4hana/ba-p/13322244

When you select multiple PIRs with Automatic Sourcing , all your PIRs become relevant for MRP.

https://me.sap.com/notes/2614920

SAP has shared the below priority in their KBA for Multiple PIRs when all have active auto-sourcing indicator.

"Purchasing info records with active auto-sourcing indicator. If several purchasing info records exist, MRP selects the one in priority: purchasing info record with given plant> purchasing info record with the smaller supplier number (LIFNR) > purchasing info record with the smaller purchasing organization (EKORG) > purchasing info record with the smaller purchasing group (EKGRP)"

For MRP you try available from and available to in PIR . Maintain and check without auto sourcing . It may help you.

 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Re: How to assign Users as Approver and Requester in SAP...
entry.content:
@ayechan ,

Based on the overview, your problem is that the recipient do not receive the form when you send the approval form, right? (this is given that all the respective user roles in BTP have been assigned properly).

Here is the thing:

the variable Approver you created should be a "List" of string. In this way, the form accepts the email addresses separately.

if you are expecting a single email/approver to pass. The script should look like this.

$context.custom.Approver =[];
$context.custom.Approver[0] = test@email123.com;

if you are expecting to have multiple approvers. You need to add condition splitting them by ";"

then pass the Approver variable to the "Users" recipient.

I hope this help.

entry.title: Re: Active/Active Read enabled option
entry.content:
Hi Imran,

Charles already gave you the probable cause for the problems, you experience here, in his first reply:

If you are trying to connect an ABAP server via a primary ABAP connection to SAP HANA, this ABAP connection will open a read/write channel to SAP HANA. This works perfectly for a primary SAP HANA instance because it’s capable of both, reading and writing to it.

But here you are trying to connect a primary ABAP connection to an AARE Secondary (Active/Active Read-Enabled) of SAP HANA System Replication which is a pure-read secondary instance, and the ABAP server can simply not open the write connection.

Unfortunately it doesn’t report this back correctly, instead he talks about missing license, probably because in the early connect process the license is checked and documented by writing to that pure-read instance which is simply not possible with a pure-read AARE secondary.

The only option to connect an ABAP server to an AARE Secondary (R/E) is via a secondary ABAP connection. That secondary ABAP connection does not need write capabilities for the connection. The primary (R/W) ABAP connection usually is sent to the corresponding primary SAP HANA instance which then can serve both, read and write.

Ralf Czekalla
SAP HANA Product Management

entry.title: Re: Unpexpected Result when export CR to pdf by API Gatew...
entry.content:
I couldn’t believe what was “wrong” with my request.

Here is my original request:

[
{
"Name": "DocKey@",
"Type": "xsd:decimal",
"Value": [
[
"1108"
]
]
}
]


And here is the request that works:

[
{
"name": "DocKey@",
"type": "xsd:decimal",
"value": [
[
"1108"
]
]
}
]

 

Problem: This request is case-sensitive, and the parameter names had to be lowercase. It took me a lot of troubleshooting time, and I would never have thought about that…

entry.title: Re: Active/Active Read enabled option
entry.content:
Hi Imran,

Charles already gave you the probable cause for the problems, you experience here, in his first reply:

If you are trying to connect an ABAP server via a primary ABAP connection to SAP HANA, this ABAP connection will open a read/write channel to SAP HANA. This works perfectly for a primary SAP HANA instance because it’s capable of both, reading and writing to it.

But here you are trying to connect a primary ABAP connection to an AARE Secondary (Active/Active Read-Enabled) of SAP HANA System Replication which is a pure-read secondary instance, and the ABAP server can simply not open the write connection.

Unfortunately it doesn’t report this back correctly, instead he talks about missing license, probably because in the early connect process the license is checked and documented by writing into that license table which is simply not possible with a pure-read AARE secondary.

The only option to connect an ABAP server to an AARE Secondary (R/E) is via a secondary ABAP connection. That secondary ABAP connection does not need write capabilities for the connection. The primary (R/W) ABAP connection usually is sent to the corresponding primary SAP HANA instance which then can serve both, read and write.

Ralf Czekalla
SAP HANA Product Management

entry.title: Re: Unleashing ABAP Potential: An introduction to Extension Libraries (XCO)
entry.content:
@tamitdassharma 

Have checked it on an ABAP Environment, S/4HANA 2023 and CAL system (S/4HANA 2022). The class XCO_LOG doesn't exist and for logging there is another API. The code would also not work:

xco_log=>factory-create_new_log

The XCO classes are build on the fluent interface pattern with static attributes. In this case the factory must be a structure and not a methode, else there should be a -> for the method 🙂

entry.title: Re: A detailed list of specific Production Planning (PP)...
entry.content:
Hi there

Thank you for this collection. However, regarding HANA it is important to always keep the Compatibility Scope in mind. I.e. while the GUI transactions for the graphical planning table might be available at the moment, their usage rights expire at the end of 2025. This applies not only to CM21 and CM29, but also MF50.
(see SAP note 2268050 - S4TWL - Graphical Planning Table)

To add more value to your list (and for ease of use) may I also suggest that you could...

  1. Add the Fiori app ID to your list(s), e.g. F0251A for Manage Material Coverage
  2. List the actual Fiori alternative to the transaction (e.g. the Fiori alternative to CM01 is Manage Work Center Capacity F3289)
    (NB: even though SAP always says that Fiori apps are not direct replacements of GUI transactions).

Lastly, you might want to revise your tags and add Production/Manufacturing (...) tags. And I am also not sure why you posted this under Q&A?

entry.title: SAP Datasphere News in November
entry.content:
One month to go towards the end of the year. Also in November we delivered great new features with our two releases. There are also lots of excellent new blogs in the community and the top features video on YouTube below.

 

My top features in November

Confluent Kafka as a source in Replication Flow

As of release 2024.23 you can now use Confluent Cloud and Confluent Platform and the corresponding connection type to add sources to replication flows. Earlier in the year we already delivered Confluent Kafka as targets in replication flows and this now closes the loop for end-to-end scenarios.
More information.

Task chain sharing to enable global task chains

You can now share task chains to other spaces where they can be nested in other task chains. This allows you to run tasks in multiple spaces orchestrated from a single task chain. In addition, you can export a task chain from its editor to a CSN/JSON file, which can then be imported into another space or tenant.
More information.

View Analyzer enhancements

The extended View Analyzer now provides data persistence guidance. This feature provides concrete recommendations for views persist to improve performance, run-time, and memory consumption. The goal is to provide readily available recommendations to optimize performance and minimize evaluation time for the business user.
More information.

Moreover, as a data modeler or data integrator, you have the option to generate an Explain Plan in the view analyzer to analyze the performance of a view and get a quick and efficient overview of your model.
More information.

Live patching of delta interval for running replication flows

We enable the live patching of delta interval for running replication flows without stopping the replication flow first. This allows you to dynamically change the delta load interval for active replication flows without restarting the data replication process.

More information.

 

 

My top blogs in November

Another great extension of the replication flow blog series. @DanielIngenhaag looks at performance of replication flows and has put together a masterpiece of a blog
➡️ Replication Flow Blog Part 7 – Performance

@DanielKraemer explains the new source connectivity with Confluent in his detailed blog  
➡️ Replication Flows Blog Series Part 8 – Confluent as a Replication Source

@JonGooding offers lots of details in his blog on 
➡️  Using Azure Private Virtual Network Endpoints with SAP Datasphere Replication

@Dominik_Graus gives great insight for scenarios where you require reading data with SAP Datasphere from the BW bridge in his blog
➡️  How to Read Data Stored in SAP Datasphere from SAP BW Bridge

Have a look at the beta program highlight of seamless planning by @Alecsandra 
➡️  Seamless Planning - Beta Program Highlights

 

More blogs to check out 👇

  • Integration architecture guide: Updated version available now
  • Using Azure Private Virtual Network Endpoints with SAP Datasphere Replication
  • How to Read Data Stored in SAP Datasphere from SAP BW Bridge
  • 2024 SAP BTP Year in Review: Release Highlights for SAP S/4HANA users
  • S/4HANA Analytics: From Fiori app to a CDS view
  • Seamless Planning - Beta Program Highlights
  • Replication Flows Blog Series Part 8 – Confluent as a Replication Source
  • SAP Integration Suiteを使ってSAP Datasphereからデータを抽出する方法 JDBC編
  • CDS view with Change Data Capture(CDC) for Replication Flow – Part 1
  • SAP Datasphere - Replication Flow (Delta Functionality)
  • Enhanced Procurement Analytics with SAP Datasphere - KAUST POC Case Study
  • Replication Flow Blog Part 7 – Performance
  • Creating a Dashboard from Address Cleansing Results using SAP Data Quality Management Microservices
  • Discover TechEd “Live” at SAPinsider EMEA 2024
  • The Definitive Guide to Connecting SAP SuccessFactors with SAP Datasphere

 

Find more information and related blog posts on the topic page for SAP Datasphere. You will find further product information on our Community with various subpages about Business Content, the SAP BW Bridge as well as content for Best Practices & Troubleshooting. Also check out the new support content for SAP Datasphere on SAP Help for troubleshooting and analysis guides, how-to guides, technical details, and more.

Find out how to unleash the power of your business data with SAP’s free learning content on SAP Datasphere. It’s designed to help you enrich your data projects, simplify the data landscape, and make the most out of your investment. Check out even more role-based learning resources and opportunities to get certified in one place on  SAP Learning site.

entry.title: Usage decision for optional characteristics
entry.content:
Hi,

I have inspection lot and do record results. The last characteristic is optional. So I recorded all results except the last which is optional.

Then I run RQEVAI30 for automatic usage decision. As result, my inspection lot does not get into the scope for automatic usage decision.

Then I go to inspection lot and "Force" the optional characteristic without any result. And now run RQEVAI30. As a result, the inspection lot got into the scope and Usage decision was done.

The question is. Is it mandatory to push "Force" for optional characteristic even if I do no need to enter any results? Is this according to standard SAP process? Or there is other possibility to make usage decision for optional characteristic not "forcing" it?

entry.title: Re: Inventory Aging Report SAP B1 (HANA)

entry.title: Re: Usage decision for optional characteristics
entry.content:
Greetings,

The inspection lots have to fulfil the following conditions:

  • All characteristics are completed.
  • No characteristic has been rejected.
  • No defects have been recorded, and no quality notification has been
    created.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Enforce a secure re-authentication for each new SAP GUI connection, with SAP Secure Login Service
entry.content:
SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI allows customers to benefit from secure authentication based on multiple factors, while at the same time improving productivity and end-user satisfaction through single sign-on. 

Most customers configure the solution so that end-users need to authenticate manually once per day. For the rest of the day, they are signed-in automatically based on the X.509 user certificate provisioned by SAP Secure Login Service. 

If customers are uncomfortable with the thought that end-users only authenticate once per day, then they can reduce the validity of the certificate to a shorter period of time. However, there are some customer scenarios where the focus is solely on the secure authentication part. These customers do not want their end-users to be signed-in automatically, not even for a limited amount of time. 

To support this requirement, we have now released a new feature that allows customers to disable single sign-on. As a result, end-users need to explicitly authenticate whenever they start a new connection in SAP GUI. 

You will find the details of the new profile setting sncForceLogin in our documentation.

entry.title: Re: SFC Card Core Plugin Extension Events Issue
entry.content:
Hi Manoel,

Thank you for your response.

I found out the solution. It is possible to trigger an event by publishing it through another extension provider.

For example, publishing PageChangeEvent from changeEquipmentStatusExtensionProvider. Since PageChangeEvent is supported by SFCCardExtension, it was captured by SFCCardExtensionProvider and it rendered new details on the SFC Card as per custom logic.

Thanks and Regards,

Sabahat Shaikh

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Upgrade: A Comprehensive Security Framework
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Upgrade: A Comprehensive Security Framework

Upgrading to SAP S/4HANA is a critical milestone for businesses seeking enhanced performance, modern features, and future-proof systems. However, amid all the planning, testing, and execution, security often takes a backseat — a risk no organization can afford.

This guide walks you through a step-by-step approach to upgrading SAP S/4HANA with security as a priority. Let’s dive into the technical roadmap to ensure your system remains robust and secure throughout the transition.

Step 1: Pre-Upgrade Assessment

1.1 Analyze the Current Security Setup

Before starting the upgrade, analyze your current SAP ECC system for potential security gaps:

  • Authorization Review: Extract and evaluate user roles and profiles using tools like SUIM or AGR_1251. Identify and eliminate inactive users and outdated roles.
  • Custom Code Security: Use tools like SAP Code Inspector (SCI) or ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) to detect vulnerabilities in custom code. This ensures compatibility with S/4HANA’s simplified data model.
  • Security Notes Compliance: Apply the latest SAP Security Notes via transaction SNOTE, and address any unresolved gaps to ensure compliance.

1.2 System Readiness Check

Run the SAP Readiness Check to uncover technical and security prerequisites, such as unsupported add-ons, custom objects, or authorization gaps. Verify compatibility with the latest SAP HANA database version.

1.3 Establish a Security Baseline

Establish a robust security baseline:

  • Compare current system parameters (rz10/rz11) with SAP's recommended values for S/4HANA.
  • Document system configurations, user roles, and access permissions as a reference for post-upgrade validation.

Step 2: Preparing for the Upgrade

2.1 Secure the System Landscape

  • Create a Sandbox Environment: Clone your production system into a sandbox for upgrade testing. Mask sensitive data to prevent unauthorized access.
  • Strengthen Network Security: Validate and restrict connections between your systems using firewalls, encryption, and secure network communication protocols.

2.2 Hardening Security Settings

  • System Parameters: Adjust parameters like login/fails_to_user_lock and login/password_expiration_time to enforce stricter authentication rules.
  • User Authentication: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for admin users and integrate Single Sign-On (SSO) through a trusted Identity Provider (IdP).

2.3 Role and Authorization Adjustments

The SU25 transaction is your go-to tool for upgrading roles and authorization objects: 

  • Synchronize existing roles with new S/4HANA transactions.
  • Identify obsolete roles or transactions and clean them up.
  • Adjust roles to include updated or newly introduced authorization objects, especially for Fiori apps.
  • Transport the Customer Table

Note:

The activities mentioned above align with the specific steps of the SU25 transaction during the SAP S/4HANA upgrade. Each step plays a crucial role in ensuring that the authorization framework is seamlessly transitioned to the upgraded system. Below is the mapping of these activities to the SU25 process:

  • Step 2A: Automatic Comparison with SU22 Data
    Compares the existing customer-specific authorization values with SAP-delivered SU22 defaults to align with the updated S/4HANA standards.
  • Step 2B: Modification Comparison with SU22 Data
    Identifies and evaluates customer-specific modifications to SAP-delivered proposals, allowing informed decisions on retaining, adapting, or discarding these changes.
  • Step 2C: Roles to Be Checked
    Highlights roles impacted by changes in transactions or authorization objects, requiring adjustments to ensure compatibility with the new system.
  • Step 2D: Search for Obsolete Applications
    Locates and flags roles or transactions linked to deprecated functionality in S/4HANA, ensuring the removal or replacement of these obsolete elements.
  • Step 3: Transport the Customer Tables
    Facilitates the migration of updated authorization objects, roles, and changes to test and production systems via standard transport mechanisms.

Step 3: Upgrade Execution

3.1 Perform Data Migration Securely

  • Encrypt system backups during data transfer using SAP Data Volume Management (DVM). Validate data integrity with checksum tools.
  • Use the Simplification Item Check to ensure custom developments and data structures align with S/4HANA.

3.2 Secure Downtime Activities

  • Enable audit logging (SM19) to monitor all activities during the upgrade.
  • Restrict system access to critical users using an access whitelist.
  • Isolate the system by temporarily disabling non-critical integrations.

3.3 Execute the Upgrade

Use SAP Software Update Manager (SUM):

  • Select security settings, such as database encryption for the HANA database.
  • Monitor SUM_Security logs to address issues with roles or security objects during the upgrade process.

Step 4: Post-Upgrade Validation

4.1 Validate Authorizations

  • Use SU53 to test for missing authorizations and resolve issues promptly.
  • Assign roles for newly introduced S/4HANA features, such as Fiori apps.

4.2 Review System Security Settings

  • Cross-check system parameters against your baseline, especially for authentication and encryption.
  • Re-enable critical background jobs (SM37) while ensuring they comply with security standards.

4.3 Patch and Secure the System

  • Update the SAP kernel and apply the latest Security Notes.
  • Perform vulnerability checks using the SAP EarlyWatch Alert and address any security concerns.

Step 5: Post-Upgrade Monitoring

5.1 Enable Continuous Monitoring

  • Leverage tools like SAP Solution Manager or SAP Enterprise Threat Detection to monitor security events in real time.
  • Configure alerts for unauthorized access or suspicious activity.

5.2 Conduct Periodic Security Audits

  • Regularly review user roles and authorizations using SUIM.
  • Use SAP Configuration Validation to ensure your system adheres to established security standards.

5.3 Educate and Train Users

  • Train users on security best practices and changes introduced by SAP S/4HANA.
  • Provide detailed documentation for administrators to manage upgraded security features effectively.

    Security

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Logging Service (CLS) API access
entry.content:
Hi @akuller_q, 

Thanks for reaching out.

The default way to read data from Cloud Logging would be to use the OpenSearch Dashboard. If you are referring to extract data directly via API of OpenSearch - we realize the benefits of access to the backend. We are working on investigating an easier provisioning of read-only-user to allow such access, minimizing the scope of database mismanagement and securing credentials. 

We will publish the timeline ones we conclude the investigation. 

Best Regards, Cloud Logging Team

entry.title: Re: From Application Logging to Cloud Logging: Service Innovation Guide
entry.content:
Hi @akuller_q, 

Thanks for reaching out. I did answer also in the other thread.

The default way to read data from Cloud Logging would be to use the OpenSearch Dashboard. To extract data directly via API of OpenSearch - we realize the benefits of access to the backend. We are working on investigating an easier provisioning of read-only-user to allow such access, minimizing the scope of database mismanagement and securing credentials.

Container metrics for Cloud Foundry applications is also in the pipeline (earlier than the API access). Using the recent contribution in Cloud Foundry community, we are currently in the load testing phase. We aim for Q1 release but it depends on the test results. 

We will publish the timeline for rollout ones we conclude the investigation. 

Best Regards, Cloud Logging Team

entry.title: Guidance for the rehire scenario
entry.content:
Hello!

I have a requirement regarding the rehire scenario of an employee for a different job/position. When rehired, the employee should retain their learning history in the LMS. One of the alternatives we found was mapping the "PersonIDexternal" field in the SF-user connector. However, when executing the integration, the information in this field is being duplicated, generating the message "Record received for duplicate entity".

We would like to know what are the best practices recommended by SAP to address this rehire scenario, ensuring that the employee's information is neither lost nor duplicated or lost.

Paul
